name: smith full squat
instructions: Set up the smith machine with the barbell at shoulder height.
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out.
Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting on your traps.
Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart.
Unrack the bar and take a step back, maintaining a stable stance.
Keeping your chest up and core engaged, initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees.
Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as your flexibility allows.
Pause for a moment at the bottom, then drive through your heels to return to the starting position.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Set up the smith machine with the barbell at shoulder height. Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart, toes slightly turned out. Step under the bar and position it across your upper back, resting on your traps. Grip the bar with your hands slightly wider than shoulder-width apart. Unrack the bar and take a step back, maintaining a stable stance. Keeping your chest up and core engaged, initiate the squat by pushing your hips back and bending your knees. Lower your body until your thighs are parallel to the ground, or as low as your flexibility allows. Pause for a moment at the bottom, then drive through your heels to return to the starting position. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Keep load symmetrical and stop at positions that create sharp pain or twisting.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Maintain neutral spine and avoid positions that aggravate the lower back.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
